reasoning: Claims >2% monthly variation in Moon angular size. This is standard celestial mechanics: the Moon's elliptical orbit (eccentricity ~0.0549) produces angular size variation of ~12% between perigee (~33.5 arcmin) and apogee (~29.4 arcmin) over the ~27.3-day anomalistic month. The >2% threshold is trivially exceeded by any model that acknowledges lunar orbital eccentricity — including standard globe-based ephemerides. The author's own status is 'suspended' with measurement methodology disputed. Data predates registration. The kernel of truth — the Moon's apparent size does vary measurably — is textbook astronomy requiring no dome framework whatsoever.
